title: bupleurum rossicum
intro: Bupleurum rossicum, also known as Butterscotch or Butterscotch Grass of Parnassus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that grows in the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and immunomodulatory effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and volatile oils. The health benefits of Bupleurum rossicum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Bupleurum rossicum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including the common cold, bronchitis, and other respiratory tract infections.
intro_study: According to "Life sciences", Bupleurum rossicum  contains saponins that have been found to be effective against TPA-induced ear edema in mice, showing anti-inflammatory properties.
